items.q: Why did Stalin reject the Marshall Plan?
items.correct: He saw it as dollar imperialism that would pull Eastern Europe into the US orbit; he set up Comecon as an Eastern bloc alternative and blocked satellite states from joining.
items.misconception: Stalin rejected the Marshall Plan because the USSR did not need money after WWII.
items.random_wrong: Stalin rejected the Marshall Plan because it required countries to fight Germany again.
items.why: The USSR was devastated by war and desperately needed aid. Stalin's refusal was strategic, not economic — accepting US dollars meant accepting US influence.

items.q: How did Henry VII secure the Tudor dynasty after 1485?
items.correct: Through marriage to Elizabeth of York, ruthless control of the nobility (bonds and recognisances), financial discipline, and crushing Yorkist pretenders like Simnel and Warbeck.
items.misconception: Henry VII was universally popular because he ended the Wars of the Roses.
items.random_wrong: Henry VII was Richard III's nephew and inherited the throne legally.
items.why: Henry's claim was weak; he ruled by control, not consent. Students assume the wars 'ended' at Bosworth, missing how shaky early Tudor rule was.

items.q: What did the 1832 Great Reform Act actually do?
items.correct: It abolished rotten boroughs, redistributed seats to industrial towns, and extended the vote to about 1 in 5 adult men — still far short of democracy.
items.misconception: The 1832 Reform Act gave the vote to all working men.
items.random_wrong: The 1832 Reform Act allowed women to vote for the first time.
items.why: Working men got the vote only in 1867 (urban) and 1884 (rural). Students compress all 19th-century franchise extensions into one act.

items.q: What was Genghis Khan's birth name?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Temüjin. “Kublai” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In which year was Temüjin proclaimed Genghis Khan at a kurultai?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 1206. “1162” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Approximately how many warriors made up a tumen?
items.why: Exact answer: 10000. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
